Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave For Neurosurgical Examination Of Plaintiff Lori Ryan
Off Calendar Apr-10-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Pavel Krepkiy, Esq. of Anna Dubrovsky Law Group, Inc., Tel. 415.746.1477 x219, for plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: Plaintiff's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MAX KNIGHT, RICHARD KNIGHT, BEVERLY KNIGHT'S Motion For Leave For Neurosurgical Examination Of Plaintiff Lori Ryan. Defendant's Motion For A Neurosurgical Examination Of Plaintiff is denied. There is insufficient showing of good cause for a second axamination of plaintiff. Plaintiff testified in her deposition, which was in advance of the first examination, that she was going to have the rhizotomy procedure to relieve the pain due to her alleged injuries and there is no showing that she is now claiming damages for a separate condition. The Court will impose no monetary sanctions. Counsel for plaintiff presented to the Court a proposed order which had been submitted to counsel for approval as to form. ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.
